title: Novartis — Financials
html:
Novartis Financial Architecture & Budget Intelligence

Governing Thought

The Novartis account is a mid-size engagement with significant unrealized potential. Revenue has been generated through a high volume of single-study HCP-Patient Conversations engagements across multiple TAs, supplemented by the Rhapsido PET tracker (the only recurring revenue stream). The account's financial architecture is fundamentally different from J&J or Merck: instead of a few large annual contracts on anchor brands, Novartis revenue is distributed across many small per-study SOWs with low individual switching cost. The single largest financial fact about this account is the gap between Novartis's commercial scale (~$53B company with brands like Entresto at ~$7.3B) and ZoomRx's penetration — three of Novartis's top-5 brands have zero ZoomRx engagement. The procurement team's file-sharing activity signals centralized vendor evaluation, which will either formalize ZoomRx's position or compress margins.

Where to Find Financial Data

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble Revenue, project-level financials, effort tracking, invoicing Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Revenue/Wins/Pipeline by cluster & account; utilization OneDrive, Base 1 file Weekly refresh
SharePoint (Novartis) Project files, deliverables, procurement-shared files ZoomRx SharePoint Per-project

How they connect: Kimble gives the ZoomRx-side truth on project revenue and effort. Cluster Management shows Novartis's share of cluster revenue. SharePoint project folders reveal project volume and activity level.

Account Financial Summary

Metric Current Estimate Notes
Active projects 4 PSP Study Proposal, SAGAN Answers demo, Procurement file sharing, Abelacimab HCP-Pt
Completed projects 20+ Across 7 years and 12+ brands
Recurring revenue Rhapsido PET (Wave 5) Only recurring tracker on the account
Revenue model Per-study SOW No annual contracts or framework agreements
AO [Populate from Kimble] --
AD [Populate from Kimble] --

[Populate actual revenue figures from Kimble — FY25 actuals, FY26 targets, and profitability data are needed to complete this section.]

Revenue by Therapeutic Area (Estimated)

Based on project count and typical pricing by service type:

Therapeutic Area Brands Served Project Count (All-Time) Estimated Revenue Contribution Status
Immunology/Derm (CSU) Remibrutinib/Rhapsido, Xolair 5+ (PET x5, HCP-Pt, Digital, CSU PET+DT) Highest — PET Wave 5 is the anchor Active
Oncology Kisqali, Pluvicto, Scemblix 6+ (HCP-Pt x3, PET x2, mHSPC HCP-Pt) Moderate — but all one-time projects Stale
Hematology PNH/ITP/wAIHA, Promacta, Fabhalta 4+ (HCP-Pt x3, legacy Promacta) Moderate — all one-time Stale
Cardiovascular Leqvio, Pelacarsen 2 (HCP-Pt x2) Low — two one-time studies Stale
Anticoagulation Abelacimab 1 (HCP-Pt + MI) Low — but active and growing Active
Gene Therapy Zolgensma 1 (Caregiver + HCP Qual) Low — single study Completed
Other Crizanlizumab, Tasigna 2 (legacy) Legacy — minimal Completed

Key observation: The Rhapsido PET alone likely accounts for 40-50% of trailing-12-month revenue. Everything else is one-time project revenue with no recurring component.

Revenue Concentration Analysis

Dimension Current State Risk Level
Rhapsido PET share of recurring revenue ~100% (only recurring tracker) HIGH
HCP-Pt share of project types ~75% of all projects HIGH (methodology concentration)
Number of brands with active work 2-3 (Rhapsido, Abelacimab, PSP) LOW brand count
Top brand dependency Rhapsido/Remibrutinib HIGH
Brands with zero engagement among top-5 3 (Entresto, Cosentyx, Kesimpta) OPPORTUNITY

Key risk: The account is effectively a Rhapsido PET account with HCP-Pt project work on the side. Losing the Rhapsido PET — whether to budget cuts, SAGAN internalization, or competitive displacement — would reduce the account to low-frequency project work with no recurring base.

Pricing Architecture

Observed Pricing by Service Type

Based on project history and cross-account benchmarks:

Service Estimated Price Range Evidence at Novartis
PET (Promotional Effectiveness Tracking) $75-150K per wave Rhapsido PET at 5 waves; Pluvicto PET; Scemblix zero-cost PET
HCP-Patient Conversations $50-100K per study 9+ HCP-Pt studies across multiple TAs
Digital Tracking $40-75K Remibrutinib Digital Tracking (completed)
Qual (HCP/Caregiver) $50-80K Zolgensma Caregiver + HCP Qual
Market Intelligence $20-40K Abelacimab MI (active)

Pricing Heuristics for Novartis

  • HCP-Pt is the entry-price service: At $50-100K per study, it fits within individual brand budgets and requires minimal procurement approval for smaller studies
  • PET is the scale-up play: Multi-wave PET (Rhapsido at Wave 5) represents the largest per-brand revenue opportunity — potentially $375-750K across 5 waves
  • Zero-cost PET was deployed on Scemblix: This suggests Novartis brand teams may expect proof-of-concept before committing to paid PET. Factor this into pricing strategy for new PET pitches.
  • Procurement involvement: The procurement file-sharing activity may introduce centralized pricing negotiation. Prepare for potential preferred vendor discount requests.

SOW Structure

Project Type Revenue Type SOW Structure Risk
Rhapsido PET Recurring (per-wave) Per-wave SOW, renewed each cycle Dependent on wave-by-wave renewal
HCP-Pt studies One-time Per-study SOW No renewal obligation
Digital Tracking Per-study Per-study SOW No renewal obligation
Qual studies One-time Per-study SOW No renewal obligation
Market Intelligence Per-study Per-study SOW No renewal obligation

Structural observation: There are no annual contracts or framework agreements at Novartis. All revenue requires active re-selling each cycle. Even the Rhapsido PET is renewed per-wave, not on an annual commitment. Converting to an annual tracking SOW (even on Rhapsido alone) would dramatically improve revenue predictability.

Novartis Market Research Budget Intelligence

What We Know

Data Point Source Confidence
Novartis total MR spend Unknown --
Brand-level MR budgets Unknown --
Preferred vendor list status Unknown — procurement activity suggests evaluation underway Low
SAGAN analytics investment Unknown — demo suggests active pilot Low
Procurement contact Unknown — folder activity detected but no named contact Low

Critical gap: No Novartis contact has shared MR budget intelligence. The procurement file-sharing activity is the strongest signal that centralized vendor evaluation is happening — but ZoomRx does not have a procurement relationship or visibility into the process.

Action items:

  • Identify the Novartis procurement contact(s) involved in vendor evaluation
  • Ask the account team whether any budget or framework agreement conversations have occurred
  • Research Novartis annual report / investor presentations for MR spending signals
  • Clarify the SAGAN Answers demo context — was this initiated by Novartis or ZoomRx? Who is the SAGAN champion?

Novartis Financial Context (Public Data)

Novartis's overall financial trajectory provides context for MR spending decisions:

Metric FY 2025 Notes
Total Revenue ~$53B [UNVERIFIED] Post-Sandoz spinoff; pure innovative medicines
Entresto ~$7.3B [UNVERIFIED] Largest brand; zero ZoomRx engagement
Cosentyx ~$4.5B [UNVERIFIED] Biosimilar risk 2027+
Kisqali ~$4B+ [UNVERIFIED] Growing on NATALEE adjuvant data
Pluvicto ~$1.5B [UNVERIFIED] Radioligand therapy; supply-constrained
R&D Investment High Pipeline-heavy strategy; Vas Narasimhan has emphasized innovation

Implication for MR budgets: Post-Sandoz, Novartis has consolidated resources into innovative medicines. MR budgets should be healthy across growth brands (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Rhapsido, Fabhalta). Mature brands (Entresto, Cosentyx) may have established vendor relationships but also face competitive threats that warrant new research. Pipeline brands (Pelacarsen, Abelacimab) are pre-launch — MR spend typically ramps during late Phase 3 and launch preparation.

Financial Watch Items

Revenue Concentration on Rhapsido (HIGH)

The Rhapsido PET is likely the majority of recurring revenue on this account. Any disruption — budget cut, SAGAN internalization of tracking analytics, or competitive displacement — would reduce the account to a collection of one-time project studies. This is the most critical financial risk on the account.

Mitigation: Extend the Rhapsido engagement (add ATU, message testing) and replicate the PET model on at least one additional brand (Kisqali PET is the strongest candidate).

Procurement Centralization Risk (MEDIUM-HIGH)

The procurement file-sharing activity is a leading indicator. If Novartis moves to a centralized vendor management model with competitive RFPs, current per-study pricing could face compression. The positive scenario: ZoomRx's 7-year track record and cross-TA versatility earn preferred vendor status. The negative scenario: ZoomRx is benchmarked against cheaper alternatives and loses on price.

Mitigation: Proactively engage procurement with a framework proposal. Position ZoomRx's cross-TA breadth and longitudinal tracking capability as differentiators that justify premium pricing. Prepare case studies demonstrating business impact across TAs.

No Annual Contracts (MEDIUM)

All Novartis revenue is per-study or per-wave with no annual commitment. This creates revenue unpredictability and requires constant re-selling.

Mitigation: Propose an annual framework agreement — even a non-binding one — that packages Rhapsido PET waves with 2-3 HCP-Pt studies across TAs at a modest volume discount. This gives Novartis procurement a clean structure and gives ZoomRx revenue visibility.

Untapped Mega-Brand Opportunity (MEDIUM — Upside)

Three of Novartis's top-5 revenue brands — Entresto ($7.3B), Cosentyx ($4.5B), and Kesimpta — have zero ZoomRx engagement. Even a single HCP-Pt study on Entresto ($75-100K) would meaningfully move account revenue. The question is not whether these brands need research — they do — but whether ZoomRx has the brand-level contacts to get in the door.

Action items:

  • Identify decision-makers on Entresto, Cosentyx, and Kesimpta brand teams
  • Prepare TA-specific HCP-Pt pitch for each (heart failure patient conversations, biosimilar defense tracking, MS self-administration tracking)
  • Use existing Novartis cross-TA credibility as the entry story

Financial Questions to Answer

Revenue & Pipeline

  • What is Novartis's trailing-12-month revenue to ZoomRx (Kimble)?
  • What is the Rhapsido PET revenue per wave and total across 5 waves?
  • What Novartis SOWs are expiring in the next 90 days and need renewal?
  • What is the current pipeline (proposals submitted but not yet won)?
  • What is the revenue from the PSP Study Proposal?

Profitability

  • What is the blended margin across Novartis projects?
  • Are any projects running over-effort (Kimble hours vs. budget)?
  • What was the cost impact of the Scemblix zero-cost PET?

Cluster Context

  • Which cluster manages Novartis?
  • What share of that cluster's total revenue comes from Novartis?
  • How does Novartis's win rate compare to other accounts in the cluster?

[Populate from Kimble pull and account team debrief.]

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
